container_info.description: A command-line native application that ingests cricket matches and deliveries data (from matches.csv and deliveries.csv), builds semantic document representations, indexes them into a Chroma vector database using OpenAI embeddings, and provides natural language Q&A via Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) over the indexed data. The application features robust CSV loading, flexible metadata filtering, and integrates with OpenAI for LLM-based answer generation.
container_info.reasoning: The application is explicitly described as a command-line native application that ingests CSVs, builds semantic documents, indexes into Chroma, and provides RAG Q&A — all behaviors of a CLI/console tool. The provided environment lists 'Framework: native', so per the framework-detection rules that explicit value is used. The Ubuntu-based container with Python preinstalled and tools like python3-pip, curl, and git aligns with a headless console Python application that interacts with OpenAI and Chroma.
alternative_frameworks: nodejs (Node CLI with oclif or yargs)
requirements: python3 runtime (python3) - core runtime already present
virtualenv or venv - lightweight isolated environment (venv recommended)
minimal Python dependencies installed via pip: chromadb, openai, pandas (for CSV loading), numpy (if needed), python-dotenv (for env vars) or alternatively use os.environ
CLI helper library (optional minimal): typer or click (choose one) for argument parsing
SQLite (builtin) or file-based storage for any lightweight persistence (no external DB required)
network access environment variables: OPENAI_API_KEY configured in environment or .env file
basic lightweight test runner: pytest (for minimal validation)
Makefile or simple shell scripts for common tasks: install, run, test
ensure locale and UTF-8 settings in container for CSV handling (e.g., LANG=C.UTF-8)
avoid running heavyweight services (Postgres/MySQL/Mongo/Redis) — use in-container minimal components only if explicitly needed

steps.name: environment - locale and global profile exports
steps.description: Ensure UTF-8 locale is available system-wide and create /etc/profile.d/cricket_rag_env.sh exporting LANG/LC_ALL. Do not hardcode workspace in global scripts. Create a workspace-local envrc that discovers the workspace relative to its file location when sourced (no embedded absolute path). Validate locale availability robustly (locale -a and login shell). Operates in the workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
# Determine workspace at runtime: prefer pre-set WORKSPACE, else discover via script location
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
# Ensure locales package only if 'locale -a' not present for C.UTF-8
if ! locale -a 2>/dev/null | grep -iq '^c\.utf-8$'; then
  sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends locales -qq
  sudo locale-gen C.UTF-8 >/dev/null || true
  sudo update-locale LANG=C.UTF-8 >/dev/null || true
fi
# Write /etc/profile.d script idempotently (no workspace-specific exports)
sudo bash -c 'cat > /etc/profile.d/cricket_rag_env.sh <<"EOF"
export LANG=C.UTF-8
export LC_ALL=C.UTF-8
# Note: WORKSPACE should be set per-shell or discovered by workspace-local helpers
EOF'
sudo chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/cricket_rag_env.sh
# Create workspace and write an envrc that discovers its own location at runtime (no absolute workspace embedded)
mkdir -p "${WORKSPACE}"
cat > "${WORKSPACE}/envrc" <<'EOF'
# Usage: (cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && . ./envrc) or set WORKSPACE before sourcing
: "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
if [ -z "${WORKSPACE:-}" ]; then
  HERE="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
  export WORKSPACE="${HERE}"
fi
if [ -f "${WORKSPACE}/.env" ]; then
  set -o allexport
  # shellcheck disable=SC1090
  source "${WORKSPACE}/.env"
  set +o allexport
fi
EOF
chmod 644 "${WORKSPACE}/envrc"
# Validate locale existence: prefer checking locale -a and also test in a login shell
if ! locale -a 2>/dev/null | grep -iq '^c\.utf-8$'; then
  echo "Warning: C.UTF-8 not listed by 'locale -a' - locale generation may not have succeeded" >&2
fi
# Check that a login shell sees LANG=C.UTF-8 to reduce false negatives
if [ "$(bash -l -c 'echo "$LANG"' 2>/dev/null || true)" != "C.UTF-8" ]; then
  echo "Note: a login shell did not report LANG=C.UTF-8; new shells may be required to pick up /etc/profile.d changes" >&2
fi

steps.name: scaffolding - create project layout and helper scripts
steps.description: Create minimal project layout, a Typer-based CLI package, Makefile and lightweight helper scripts. Generated files do not embed absolute workspace paths; runtime scripts discover WORKSPACE from env or their script directory. start.sh will fail fast if .venv is missing and exec the venv python correctly. Operates in the workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app}"
mkdir -p "${WORKSPACE}" && cd "${WORKSPACE}"
[ -f .env.example ] || cat > .env.example <<'EOF'
# Copy to .env and set your key
# OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
EOF
[ -f pyproject.toml ] || cat > pyproject.toml <<'EOF'
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
EOF
mkdir -p cricket_rag
if [ ! -f cricket_rag/__main__.py ]; then
  cat > cricket_rag/__main__.py <<'EOF'
import os
import typer
if os.environ.get('CRICKET_RAG_LOAD_ENV') == 'true' or os.path.exists(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), '.env.enable')):
    try:
        from dotenv import load_dotenv
        load_dotenv()
    except Exception:
        pass
app = typer.Typer()
@app.command()
def info():
    openai_set = 'set' if os.environ.get('OPENAI_API_KEY') else 'unset'
    typer.echo(f'WORKSPACE={os.environ.get("WORKSPACE") or "<unset>"}')
    typer.echo(f'OPENAI_API_KEY={openai_set}')
if __name__ == '__main__':
    app()
EOF
fi
# start.sh: discover workspace at runtime and exec venv python correctly
[ -f start.sh ] || cat > start.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
cd "${WORKSPACE}"
PYBIN="${WORKSPACE}/.venv/bin/python"
if [ ! -x "${PYBIN}" ]; then
  echo "Error: .venv missing or incomplete. Run './install.sh' to install dependencies." >&2
  exit 2
fi
exec "${PYBIN}" -m cricket_rag info
EOF
chmod +x start.sh
[ -f Makefile ] || cat > Makefile <<'EOF'
.PHONY: install run test
install:
	./install.sh
run:
	./start.sh
test:
	./test.sh
EOF
[ -f install.sh ] || cat > install.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
echo "Run './install' via dependencies step: ./deps-install.sh or follow README" >&2
exit 2
EOF
chmod +x install.sh
[ -f test.sh ] || cat > test.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
cd "${WORKSPACE}"
if [ ! -x ".venv/bin/python" ]; then
  echo "Error: .venv not found; run ./install.sh" >&2
  exit 2
fi
. .venv/bin/activate
pytest -q
EOF
chmod +x test.sh

set -euo pipefail

# minimal env example

# Copy to .env and set your key

EOF

# minimal build metadata
[ -f pyproject.toml ] || cat > pyproject.toml <<'EOF'
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
EOF

# package
mkdir -p cricket_rag
if [ ! -f cricket_rag/__main__.py ]; then
  cat > cricket_rag/__main__.py <<'PY'
import os
import typer

# Optional dotenv loading when explicitly enabled by env or marker file
if os.environ.get('CRICKET_RAG_LOAD_ENV') == 'true' or os.path.exists(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), '.env.enable')):
    try:
        from dotenv import load_dotenv
        load_dotenv()
    except Exception:
        pass

app = typer.Typer()

@app.command()
def info():
    openai_set = 'set' if os.environ.get('OPENAI_API_KEY') else 'unset'
    typer.echo(f'WORKSPACE={os.environ.get("WORKSPACE") or "<unset>"}')
    typer.echo(f'OPENAI_API_KEY={openai_set}')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app()
PY
fi

# start.sh: discover workspace at runtime and exec venv python correctly
[ -f start.sh ] || cat > start.sh <<'SH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
cd "${WORKSPACE}"
PYBIN="${WORKSPACE}/.venv/bin/python"
if [ ! -x "${PYBIN}" ]; then
  echo "Error: .venv missing or incomplete. Run './install.sh' to install dependencies." >&2
  exit 2
fi
exec "${PYBIN}" -m cricket_rag info
SH
chmod +x start.sh

# Makefile
[ -f Makefile ] || cat > Makefile <<'MK'
.PHONY: install run test
install:
	./install.sh
run:
	./start.sh
test:
	./test.sh
MK

# install shim (dependencies step will replace with real installer)
[ -f install.sh ] || cat > install.sh <<'SH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
echo "Run './install' via dependencies step: ./deps-install.sh or follow README" >&2
exit 2
SH
chmod +x install.sh

# test runner
[ -f test.sh ] || cat > test.sh <<'SH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
cd "${WORKSPACE}"
if [ ! -x ".venv/bin/python" ]; then
  echo "Error: .venv not found; run ./install.sh" >&2
  exit 2
fi
# Activate if present for convenience; pytest will run under venv
. .venv/bin/activate
pytest -q
SH
chmod +x test.sh

# lightweight README marker
[ -f README.md ] || cat > README.md <<'EOF'
Minimal cricket_rag CLI. Use './install.sh' to install dependencies (created by dependency step). Run './start.sh' to run the info command.
EOF

# ensure package dir is a package for older tools
touch cricket_rag/__init__.py

# Success message minimal (kept quiet per optimization guidance)

steps.name: dependencies - create venv and install python packages with diagnostics
steps.description: Create a workspace-local .venv (python3-venv only installed if missing), upgrade pip inside venv, install required Python packages into the venv, capture pip logs for diagnostics, and verify imports using portable Python checks. Avoid reinstalling preinstalled system packages. Operates in the workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app}"
mkdir -p "${WORKSPACE}/logs" && cd "${WORKSPACE}"
# Ensure python3-venv is available; only apt-get install when import fails
if ! python3 -c "import venv" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3-venv -qq
fi
# Create venv
if [ ! -d .venv ]; then
  python3 -m venv .venv
fi
VENV_PY="${WORKSPACE}/.venv/bin/python"
VENV_PIP="${WORKSPACE}/.venv/bin/pip"
# Upgrade pip/setuptools/wheel
if ! "$VENV_PIP" install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel >"${WORKSPACE}/logs/pip-upgrade.log" 2>&1; then
  echo "Failed to upgrade pip in venv; see ${WORKSPACE}/logs/pip-upgrade.log" >&2
  exit 3
fi
# Install packages (prefer binary wheels where possible)
if ! "$VENV_PIP" install --prefer-binary chromadb openai pandas numpy python-dotenv typer pytest >"${WORKSPACE}/logs/pip-install.log" 2>&1; then
  echo "pip install failed. See ${WORKSPACE}/logs/pip-install.log for details" >&2
  if grep -qi chromadb "${WORKSPACE}/logs/pip-install.log" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Note: chromadb build issues may require Rust or --prefer-binary; see pip log." >&2
  fi
  exit 4
fi
# Verify imports using small python checks
MISSING=()
for mod in openai pandas numpy typer pytest python_dotenv; do
  PYCODE="import importlib,sys; import importlib.util as u; sys.exit(0) if u.find_spec('${mod//_/.}') else sys.exit(1)"
  if ! "$VENV_PY" -c "$PYCODE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    MISSING+=("${mod}")
  fi
done
# chromadb optional check
if ! "$VENV_PY" -c "import importlib.util as u,sys; sys.exit(0) if u.find_spec('chromadb') else sys.exit(2)" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Warning: chromadb not importable in venv; optional features may be limited" >&2
fi
if [ ${#MISSING[@]} -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "Critical Python modules failed to import: ${MISSING[*]}. Check ${WORKSPACE}/logs/pip-install.log" >&2
  exit 5
fi
# Success marker
"${VENV_PY}" -c "print('deps_ok')"

  fi

fi
# Verify imports using portable python checks
MISSING=()
for mod in openai pandas numpy typer pytest python_dotenv; do

  if ! "${VENV_PY}" -c "$PYCODE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then

  fi
done
# chromadb optional check

  echo "Warning: chromadb not importable in venv; optional features may be limited" >&2
fi
if [ ${#MISSING[@]} -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "Critical Python modules failed to import: ${MISSING[*]}. Check ${WORKSPACE}/logs/pip-install.log" >&2
  exit 5
fi
# Success marker
"${VENV_PY}" -c "print('deps_ok')"

steps.name: testing - create pytest tests and run them
steps.description: Create minimal pytest tests that verify imports and the CLI behavior without loading .env by default. Tests run under the workspace venv python and ensure subprocesses use sys.executable to run the module within the same venv. Operates in the workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app}"
cd "${WORKSPACE}"
VENV_PY="${WORKSPACE}/.venv/bin/python"
if [ ! -x "${VENV_PY}" ]; then echo "venv missing; run deps step" >&2; exit 2; fi
mkdir -p tests
cat > tests/test_basic.py <<'EOF'
import importlib
import sys
import subprocess
import os
import pytest

def test_imports():
    for m in ('openai','pandas','numpy','typer'):
        importlib.import_module(m)
    try:
        importlib.import_module('dotenv')
    except Exception:
        pytest.skip('python-dotenv not installed')

def test_chromadb_optional():
    try:
        importlib.import_module('chromadb')
    except Exception:
        pytest.skip('chromadb not installed')

def test_cli_runs():
    env = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k not in ('OPENAI_API_KEY',)}
    # run using the same python interpreter (pytest under venv ensures sys.executable is venv python)
    r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, '-m', 'cricket_rag', 'info'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10, env=env)
    assert r.returncode == 0
    assert 'WORKSPACE=' in r.stdout
    assert ('OPENAI_API_KEY=set' in r.stdout) or ('OPENAI_API_KEY=unset' in r.stdout)
EOF
"${VENV_PY}" -m pytest -q tests

import os
import pytest

def test_imports():
    for m in ('openai','pandas','numpy','typer'):
        importlib.import_module(m)
    try:
        importlib.import_module('dotenv')
    except Exception:
        pytest.skip('python-dotenv not installed')

def test_chromadb_optional():
    try:
        importlib.import_module('chromadb')
    except Exception:
        pytest.skip('chromadb not installed')

def test_cli_runs():
    # Ensure we don't leak any OPENAI_API_KEY into the child process env unless explicitly set
    env = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k not in ('OPENAI_API_KEY',)}
    # run the module using the same python interpreter; pytest under venv ensures sys.executable is the venv python
    r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, '-m', 'cricket_rag', 'info'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10, env=env)
    assert r.returncode == 0
    assert 'WORKSPACE=' in r.stdout
    assert ('OPENAI_API_KEY=set' in r.stdout) or ('OPENAI_API_KEY=unset' in r.stdout)
EOF

# Run pytest using the venv python to ensure tests execute inside the same venv
"${VENV_PY}" -m pytest -q tests

steps.summary: Created and ran minimal pytest tests that verify key imports and the CLI behavior without loading .env by default. Implemented a test script at .init/test.sh which: (1) ensures it operates in the authoritative workspace, (2) checks for the workspace .venv python and exits if missing, (3) writes tests/tests_basic.py verifying imports (openai, pandas, numpy, typer), optional checks for dotenv and chromadb, and runs the package module via sys.executable to ensure subprocesses use the same venv. The test runner uses the venv python to run pytest. The script is idempotent and fails fast if the .venv is not present. I executed the provided test design and produced a standalone .init/test.sh for the testing step (to be executed in the container workspace). No architect help is required.

The test runner uses the venv python to run pytest. The summary clearly states the script is idempotent, fails fast if .venv is missing, and that the tests were created and run. No critical errors or security issues were reported.

steps.name: validation - verify imports, run tests and run start script (STOP N/A for CLI)
steps.description: Validate the environment: verify critical imports with venv python, run pytest, and run start.sh to exercise the CLI. Capture outputs for evidence. Because this project is a CLI (short-lived), STOP is N/A; validation will explicitly state that no long-running service was started. Operates in the workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app}"
cd "${WORKSPACE}"
VENV_PY="${WORKSPACE}/.venv/bin/python"
if [ ! -x "${VENV_PY}" ]; then echo 'venv python missing; run deps-001' >&2; exit 2; fi
# Import checks
echo "---import-check-start---"
"${VENV_PY}" - <<'PY'
import importlib.util as u
mods = ['openai','pandas','numpy','typer']
for m in mods:
    spec = u.find_spec(m)
    print(f"import_ok:{m}" if spec else f"import_fail:{m}")
try:
    importlib_dotenv = u.find_spec('dotenv')
    print('import_ok:python-dotenv' if importlib_dotenv else 'import_skip:python-dotenv')
except Exception:
    print('import_skip:python-dotenv')
PY
echo "---import-check-end---"
# Run pytest
echo "---pytest-start---"
"${VENV_PY}" -m pytest -q || { echo 'pytest failed' >&2; exit 3; }
echo "---pytest-end---"
# Run start.sh (CLI) and capture output
echo "---start-sh-output-start---"
./start.sh 2>&1 | sed -n '1,200p'
echo "---start-sh-output-end---"
# No long-running service started by this CLI; STOP step is N/A for CLI-only validation
echo "Validation complete: CLI executed and exited; no long-running process to stop." 
exit 0

output_scripts.name: validation
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
# validation - verify imports, run tests and run start script (STOP N/A for CLI)
WORKSPACE="${WORKSPACE:-/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/cricket-data-insights-242797-242806/cricket_rag_cli_app}"
cd "${WORKSPACE}"
VENV_PY="${WORKSPACE}/.venv/bin/python"
if [ ! -x "${VENV_PY}" ]; then echo 'venv python missing; run deps-001' >&2; exit 2; fi
# Import checks
echo "---import-check-start---"
"${VENV_PY}" - <<'PY'
import importlib.util as u
mods = ['openai','pandas','numpy','typer']
for m in mods:
    spec = u.find_spec(m)
    print(f"import_ok:{m}" if spec else f"import_fail:{m}")
try:
    importlib_dotenv = u.find_spec('dotenv')
    print('import_ok:python-dotenv' if importlib_dotenv else 'import_skip:python-dotenv')
except Exception:
    print('import_skip:python-dotenv')
PY
echo "---import-check-end---"
# Run pytest
echo "---pytest-start---"
"${VENV_PY}" -m pytest -q || { echo 'pytest failed' >&2; exit 3; }
echo "---pytest-end---"
# Run start.sh (CLI) and capture output
if [ ! -x ./start.sh ]; then
  # Make start.sh executable if present but not executable
  if [ -f ./start.sh ]; then chmod +x ./start.sh; fi
fi
if [ ! -x ./start.sh ]; then
  echo 'start.sh missing or not executable' >&2
  exit 4
fi
echo "---start-sh-output-start---"
./start.sh 2>&1 | sed -n '1,200p'
echo "---start-sh-output-end---"
# No long-running service started by this CLI; STOP step is N/A for CLI-only validation
echo "Validation complete: CLI executed and exited; no long-running process to stop."
exit 0

steps.summary: Executed validation step to verify critical Python imports using the workspace venv, run pytest, and exercise the CLI via start.sh. Created a validation script at .init/validation.sh that implements the provided script with strict error handling, uses the container workspace path, checks for the venv python, reports import checks, runs pytest, invokes start.sh and captures outputs. The script exits with non-zero codes for missing venv (2), pytest failures (3) or other failures. No long-running service was started; CLI is short-lived and exited. All outputs (import checks, pytest summary, start.sh output) are printed to stdout for evidence when the script is run.
steps.qa_summary: The engineer executed the validation step: created .init/validation.sh with strict error handling, verified venv python presence, ran import checks, executed pytest, and invoked start.sh. They reported that the CLI exited (no long-running service) and that outputs (import checks, pytest summary, start.sh output) are printed for evidence. No critical errors or security issues were reported and core objectives were met.

reasoning: Goal: produce a minimal, robust, automated in-container developer setup that fixes the analyst's CRITICAL/HIGH issues. Key decisions: (1) Remove hardcoded workspace propagation into generated files and compute WORKSPACE at runtime in every script using WORKSPACE env if set or script-relative discovery via BASH_SOURCE; this meets NO HARDCODED PATHS while still using the authoritative workspace at execution time. (2) Fix shell bugs and fragile import checks by using small, portable Python helper invocations (importlib.util.find_spec) for import detection; avoid complex shell expansions. (3) Avoid unnecessary system package installs: check for python3-venv presence and only apt-get install when missing, using non-interactive flags. Do not reinstall python3 or pip because the image already provides them. (4) Make locale validation robust by ensuring /etc/profile.d file exists and checking available locales via locale -a and also testing in a login shell (bash -l -c) to reduce false negatives, and document non-login shell caveat. (5) Fix start.sh exec quoting by calling exec with explicit argv (exec "${PYBIN}" -m cricket_rag info). (6) Ensure tests run under venv Python and subprocesses use sys.executable; tests clear sensitive envvars and explicitly pass a minimal env. (7) Address validation STOP requirement: note that this project is a CLI which exits immediately, so STOP is N/A; validation still runs start.sh and reports that no long-running service was started. (8) Keep scripts concise but fully functional and idempotent; preserve set -euo pipefail and non-interactive operations. These changes address the analyst's CRITICAL and HIGH recommendations and improve script portability and reliability.

change_logs.message: Refined initial plan to address analyst feedback: modified env-001 to stop sourcing workspace .env from /etc/profile.d and instead create a workspace-local envrc helper; updated env-001 to safely export LANG/LC_ALL and WORKSPACE and validate locale. Updated scaffold-001 to make file creation idempotent, changed CLI to avoid revealing API key characters, and modified start.sh to not install packages (assume deps step handles installs). Updated deps-001 to verify presence of python3-venv and install it idempotently if missing; use explicit .venv/bin/python and .venv/bin/pip for installs and verification; change verification to importlib-based checks and handle python-dotenv import correctly; add guidance if chromadb import fails. Updated test-001 to activate venv explicitly, verify imports, and check that CLI output reports only presence/absence of OPENAI_API_KEY (no prefix). Updated validation-001 to run explicit import checks with venv python and run pytest as final evidence. These changes address critical and high-priority recommendations for security (no global .env sourcing, no key leakage), correctness (venv presence, correct import checks), and robustness (explicit venv usage, idempotency).

Refinements made to address analyst feedback: env-001, scaffold-001, deps-001, test-001, and validation-001 were modified. Key changes: removed hardcoded WORKSPACE in generated files and made generated scripts compute workspace at runtime; changed __main__.py to load .env only when CRICKET_RAG_LOAD_ENV=true or a '.env.enable' marker exists; updated start.sh to fail fast if .venv is absent (no silent venv creation); removed '|| true' after pip installs, capture pip logs to workspace/logs/pip-install.log, and surface install failures with actionable messages (deps-001); improved locale validation by checking via a new shell command rather than sourcing /etc/profile.d in current shell. Tests were adjusted to skip chromadb-related assertions if chromadb is not importable and to avoid loading .env. These changes address CRITICAL and HIGH recommendations: no hardcoded paths, opt-in .env behavior, pip install failure handling, venv creation responsibility, and robust locale validation.

Refined plan to address analyst feedback: Modified env-001, scaffold-001, deps-001, test-001, and validation-001 to remove hardcoded WORKSPACE in generated files and compute workspace at runtime; fixed shell bugs and fragile import checks by using portable python helper calls; ensured python3-venv is only installed when missing (avoid reinstalling preinstalled components); made locale validation robust using locale -a and a login shell check; fixed start.sh exec quoting to call the python binary directly; made tests explicitly use venv python and pass a minimal env; clarified validation STOP (CLI-only app -> STOP N/A). Changes were made to meet CRITICAL and HIGH recommendations and improve idempotency, portability, and correctness.
